• Home

Cue Shift

This is not my first VR project, but it is the one with the most creative freedom. For a course at my university, I am building a custom locomotion and interaction system in Unity. The goal is to avoid the obvious answers. Teleportation? Too straightforward. Grabbing objects with your hands? Every VR tutorial covers that. The course demands something different, so the aim is to rethink two things from scratch: how the player moves through the world, and how they interact with objects in it.

A description of the project requirements, how a mobile game became the inspiration for this project, and the ideation process that led to the final concept.

Modifying the Map

Adapting a parkour map template to fit the pool theme, figuring out how to use a game engine as a 3D modeling tool and restricting the player to the playable area.

Read More

The Cue Stick

Spawning a cue stick in the scene, implementing a two-handed grip system with a focus on accessibility.

Read More

Locomotion System

Constructing the locomotion system, by allowing the player to orbit around the ball, setting up a shot and striking.

Read More

Interaction System

Creating an interaction system that allows the player to manipulate an object's position and rotation by using the cue stick.

Read More

Playthrough, User Testing and Feedback

A complete playthrough of the final experience, along with scores from other users who tested it and their feedback.

Closing Thoughts and AI Disclosure

A retrospective on the course and the role of AI in its development of this project.
